compiled_state.title: Create remaining 21 wrapper command files
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Create .claude/commands/ wrapper files for all remaining user-invocable skills (21 files) using the same template pattern validated in the core wrappers task.

Skills to create wrappers for: architect-foundation, design-onboarding, design-review, dev-onboarding, dryrun, end-session, fix-cascade-recovery, generate-claude-md, implementation-plan, init, interface-contracts, onboarding, playwright-debug, project-setup, quick-ref, resolve-conflicts, retro, roadmap, tdd-workflow, tickets-health, update-docs.

Each uses the identical detection template from the core wrappers task, substituting only the skill name.

### TDD Exemption
Unit test exempt. Justification: (1) No conditional logic — each wrapper is identical template with skill name substituted, (2) any test would be a change-detector test asserting file existence, (3) infrastructure-boundary-only — static markdown files with no behavioral logic. The detection pattern was already validated by tests in the prior task.

### File Impact
- .claude/commands/architect-foundation.md through .claude/commands/update-docs.md (21 files, create)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] All 26 wrapper files exist (5 core + 21 remaining)
  Verify: test $(ls .claude/commands/*.md 2>/dev/null | wc -l) -ge 26
- [ ] Each wrapper references its correct skill name
  Verify: for f in .claude/commands/*.md; do name=$(basename "$f" .md); grep -q "dso:${name}" "$f" || echo "MISSING: $name"; done 2>&1 | grep -c MISSING | awk '{exit ($1 > 0)}'
- [ ] Each wrapper contains detection logic
  Verify: for f in .claude/commands/*.md; do grep -q 'PLUGIN_DETECTED' "$f" || echo "NO_DETECT: $(basename $f)"; done 2>&1 | grep -c NO_DETECT | awk '{exit ($1 > 0)}'
